title: rubus silvaticus subsp. debilispinus
intro: Rubus silvaticus subsp. debilispinus is a subspecies of bramble, a type of wild raspberry native to certain regions, characterized by its slender stems and small, edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Rubus silvaticus subsp. debilispinus include its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds, digestive issues, and skin problems. The health benefits of consuming Rubus silvaticus subsp. debilispinus include its high fiber content, which can help regulate bowel movements and support healthy digestion, as well as its antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Rubus silvaticus subsp. debilispinus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
